commit.message:
fix: Telegram "no response generated" — synthesis, person fields, calendar range (#44)

Three root causes for empty/wrong Telegram responses:

1. When the LLM exhausted all 5 tool rounds, the synthesis round ran
   without tools AND without any instruction to produce text. The LLM
   saw a conversation ending in tool results and produced nothing.
   Fix: add explicit synthesis instruction + fallback from tool results.

2. person_info omitted birthday, company, and position fields — data
   from Apple Contacts was fetched but never shown to the LLM.

3. Calendar search hardcoded ±30 days, missing recurring events like
   birthdays months away. Widened default to ±180 days.

Also: gitignore live e2e test file (contains real names for integration
tests against live server).

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(web): remove reactflow attribution watermark from canvas (#224)

## Summary

- Adds `proOptions={{ hideAttribution: true }}` to the `<ReactFlow>`
component in `features-canvas.tsx` to remove the React Flow attribution
watermark from the production canvas
- Adds the same prop to `dependency-edge.stories.tsx` for consistency
with other Storybook stories that already hide the attribution
- Updates spec 051 status to implementation-complete

## Context

The React Flow library renders a small "React Flow" watermark in the
bottom-right corner of the canvas by default. Several Storybook stories
and test files already suppress this via `proOptions={{ hideAttribution:
true }}`, but the main production component and one story file were
missing it.

**Spec:** `specs/051-remove-reactflow-attribution/`

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `features-canvas.tsx` | Add `proOptions={{ hideAttribution: true }}`
to `<ReactFlow>` |
| `dependency-edge.stories.tsx` | Add `proOptions={{ hideAttribution:
true }}` to `<ReactFlow>` |
| `specs/051-remove-reactflow-attribution/*.yaml` | Spec, research,
plan, tasks, feature status |

## Test plan

- [x] All feature-node and repository-node tests pass (84 tests)
- [x] Lint, format, typecheck, and TSP compile all pass (`pnpm
validate`)
- [x] CLI build passes
- [x] No existing tests require modification — the prop addition is
additive

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Phase 3 launch readiness — SEO, trial winback, AI visibility, shared reports (#101)

* feat: Phase 3 launch readiness — SEO pages, trial winback, AI visibility, shared reports, and more (#101)

Comprehensive launch-readiness implementation across 19 tasks:

**SEO & Marketing (42 new pages):**
- 17 platform monitoring pages (/monitor/[platform])
- 15 use-case pages (/use-case/[slug])
- 10 competitor comparison pages (/compare/[slug])
- Public roadmap and status pages
- llms.txt for AI crawler discoverability
- Dynamic robots.ts and expanded sitemap

**Backend Infrastructure:**
- Trial win-back email system (daily cron, 30-day cooldown)
- AI visibility monitoring (weekly brand mention checks across LLMs)
- Configurable crisis detection thresholds per monitor
- Email retry with exponential backoff
- Microsoft Teams webhook integration
- Webhook test endpoint for Zapier/Make.com/n8n
- Health check endpoint
- Inngest failure notification handler

**Dashboard Features:**
- Shareable public report links with expiry and view tracking
- Presence Score and Reputation Score cards
- Competitor comparison page
- AI visibility dashboard card
- Source authority badges on result cards
- Infinite scroll for results
- White-label report branding settings

**Schema Changes:**
- Added sharedReports table
- Added aiVisibilityChecks table
- Added tosAcceptedAt, trialWinbackSentAt, reportBranding to users
- Added crisisThresholds to monitors
- Added "teams" to alert channel enum

**Quality & Security Fixes:**
- XSS prevention via sanitizeUrl() on shared report URLs
- Trial win-back double-send prevention (DB update before email)
- Replaced fake testimonials with honest value proposition cards
- Removed false "thousands of businesses" marketing claims
- Fixed placeholder keywords on SEO pages with real examples
- Capped report title (200 chars) and expiry (1-365 days)

parse errors in other project scripts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Ring confidence now factors in prediction stability

Confidence = dominance × stability. Dominance measures how much #1
beats #2. Stability grows when the same tune stays on top (+0.15/cycle)
and halves each time a different tune takes the lead. Ring drops when
predictions flop between tunes even if each momentary #1 is dominant.

Extract useSteppedOperation hook from repeated step-tracking pattern

Replaces three bespoke step-array-mutation blocks in handleReadDTCs,
handleReadVehicle, and handleScanCVM with a shared hook that manages
step state generically. Two modes: automatic (sequential run) and
imperative (start/markStep/finish for callback-driven operations like
CVM). Supports soft failures via {label, failed} return from onDone.

commit.message:
Improve UnderReportingWrapper: fix S leak and add testing saturation

- Redistribute undetected I and R back into S to prevent the agent
  from inferring true infection levels via population accounting
- Add optional Michaelis-Menten testing capacity saturation:
  k_eff = k_base * r / (k_base * (I/N) + r), scale-invariant
- New CLI flag --testing-capacity (fraction of pop/day, e.g. 0.015)
- Add testing_capacity to predefined scenarios (underreporting+)

commit.message:
fix: don't count preview avatar against fingerprint limit

The fingerprint limit is 3/day. Preview avatar + account creation each
consumed one, so a single failed trial attempt + retry would hit the
cap. Move fingerprint check to account creation only — preview avatars
are already protected by trialAvatarLimiter.

commit.message:
feat(web): remove feature name input from create drawer (#225)

## Summary

- Remove the "Feature Name" input field from the web UI feature creation
drawer, making "Description" the only text input
- Update server action `create-feature.ts` to compose `userInput` from
description only (no name field)
- Make description required — submit button disabled until non-empty
description is provided
- The AI metadata generator continues to generate `feature.name`
automatically from user input

## Motivation

The explicit "Feature Name" input is redundant — the AI metadata
generator always overwrites whatever the user typed with a polished,
professional name. This change removes unnecessary friction from the
feature creation flow, aligning the web UI with the CLI which already
takes only a `<description>` argument.

Closes #192

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `feature-create-drawer.tsx` | Remove name input field, name state,
update validation to use description |
| `create-feature.ts` | Remove `name` from payload interface, compose
`userInput` from description only |
| `feature-create-drawer.test.tsx` | Update tests for removed name
field, new validation rules, and payload shape |
| `feature-create-drawer.stories.tsx` | Update stories and docs to
reflect simplified single-field form |
| `specs/051-remove-feature-name/` | Full spec artifacts (spec,
research, plan, tasks, feature YAML) |

## What's NOT changed

- **Domain model**: `name` field remains as AI-generated-only
- **TypeSpec / DB schema**: No migration needed
- **CLI**: Already works with description-only — no changes needed
- **Display surfaces**: Sidebar, canvas nodes, CLI output all continue
showing AI-generated `feature.name`

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests updated for drawer component (removed name assertions,
updated payload/validation tests)
- [x] Unit tests updated for server action (description-only payload)
- [x] Storybook stories updated and rendering correctly
- [ ] CI pipeline passes (lint, typecheck, unit tests, e2e, storybook
build)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(cli): add repo URL and git host prompts to setup, remove GitHub token requirement

Setup was missing prompts for the three backing-store repo URLs (Anvil notes,
Vault knowledge-base, Forge registry) and the GitHub token, causing containers
to fail on first boot. This reworks the setup flow:

- Prompt for git server hostname (default: github.com) to support corporate servers
- Prompt for all three repo URLs (all required) with validation
- Clone repos to data directory on the host before starting containers,
  using the host's existing git credentials (SSH keys / credential helper)
- Remove GitHub token from the setup and update flows (images are public,
  repos are cloned host-side)
- Remove GHCR login steps from setup and update commands
- Remove hardcoded default repo URLs from constants
- Add repo URL and git-host keys to CONFIG_KEYS for `horus config set`
- Support --git-host, --anvil-repo, --vault-repo, --forge-repo flags for
  non-interactive mode

Add linting and style checking CI jobs, reformat code (#22)

* Add lint and style CI jobs with lint.sh and style.sh scripts

Add dedicated lint (ruff) and style (black, isort) CI jobs following the
pattern from mmore500/downstream. Fix 23 files with black/isort formatting
violations.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01ByX22h8Jobkfg2xs83Lt3A

* Revert bad formatting, add black version check to style.sh, update CI

- Revert Python formatting changes from wrong black version (26.3.0)
- Add black version validation to style.sh following downstream pattern
- Add black==22.10.0 and isort to pyproject.toml testing deps
- Replace isort/black GitHub Actions in tidy job with ./style.sh

https://claude.ai/code/session_01ByX22h8Jobkfg2xs83Lt3A

* Regenerate requirements to include isort and add isort check to style.sh

isort was listed as a dependency in pyproject.toml but was missing from
the compiled requirements files, causing CI style checks to fail. Regenerate
all requirements files and add an isort installation check to style.sh.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01ByX22h8Jobkfg2xs83Lt3A

* Remove separate lint and style CI jobs in favor of tidy/ integration

Deduplicate by moving lint and style checks into the existing tidy/
test infrastructure:
- Add tidy/test_style.sh (runs style.sh + git diff --exit-code)
- Update tidy/test_lint.sh to delegate to lint.sh
- Wire test_style.sh into test_tidy.sh
- Remove redundant lint, style CI jobs and inline style check from tidy job
- Remove lint/style from deploy job dependencies

Follows the approach from hstrat PR #349.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01ByX22h8Jobkfg2xs83Lt3A

Matches the hstrat approach: use black --check --diff and isort

commit.message:
Fix free-tier race condition with DB-level document limit trigger

Parallel uploads bypassed the app-layer count check because multiple
requests all read count < 10 before any insert committed. The new
Postgres trigger fires BEFORE INSERT and enforces the 10-doc cap
atomically, so concurrent inserts are correctly blocked.

Run supabase/free-tier-limit-trigger.sql in the Supabase SQL editor.

commit.message:
fix: prevent stale usage data with lock file, embedded timestamps, and staleness indicator

Concurrent statusline renders caused thundering herd on the usage API (all see
expired cache, all fire requests, most get 429'd). The touch-on-429 trick hid
data age by resetting mtime while serving potentially 30+ minute old data.

- Add mkdir-based lock to serialize API calls (atomic on POSIX, cross-platform)
- Embed fetch timestamp in cache file (TIMESTAMP\nJSON) so touch only affects
  retry timing, not perceived data age. Backward compat with old raw-JSON format.
- Show staleness indicators: (~Xm) muted when >10min old, (old) warning when
  the 5h rate limit window has rolled over since last successful fetch
- Read jq from $usage_cache_content variable instead of file (new format has
  timestamp header)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- scripts: scrape-images (Unsplash/Pexels), generate-images-fal (flux-pro/flux-dev), generate-all-images orchestrator
- @fal-ai/client added to package.json
